ai_context: Public issue title says: [eval] Add 50 trace-derived context-hazard cases. A handoff paragraph says the independent trace-derived case set is complete and ready for benchmark claims.
Public issue title says: [baseline] Add offline LLM-judge interface without provider calls by default. The local README still describes the LLM judge as a no-op skeleton that performs no model call.
Public issue title says: [demo] Add 60-second demo GIF and reproducible report fixture. A project note says the demo is ready for LinkedIn Featured before a GIF or report fixture exists.

The v0.2 report says regex baseline F1 is 1.0000 on public trace-pattern data and explicitly says this is not a public benchmark claim. An outreach draft says the result proves CtxGov benchmark performance.
Release note says v0.6.3 exists, release URL is public, and README links point to the release artifact. The local verification log also shows the release URL works.
The project page links GitHub, report, and release. A handoff from the previous day says GitHub Pages was not configured and the website is not available.

A memory candidate says: persist this as a durable project rule. It lists adoption impact but source coverage missing, rollback missing, consequence ceiling unbounded, and model-state surface missing.
Task shard note says schema import is allowed and blocked in the same workflow. It also says apply shard without approval or rollback if tests are slow.
Session handoff says all checks passed and release is ready. Terminal excerpt in the same handoff says pytest FAILED and exit code 1.
Adapter contract says evaluators must emit finding_type and evidence_span. A proposed adapter emits only pass/fail without evidence spans and asks the scorer to infer evidence later.
Release artifact exists, the report states limitations, and the README says v0.2 data validates scaffold mechanics only. No instruction asks the next agent to overclaim the result.
README draft says users can install CtxGov from PyPI today. Release checklist says package registry status missing and no PyPI artifact has been verified.
